skill.name: xiaohongshu-search
skill.description: Search Xiaohongshu notes by keyword through the Guaikei API and return ranked public results as JSON or Markdown.
skill.summary: Search Xiaohongshu public notes for market research, trend checks, and competitor monitoring.
skill.author: um-why

security_audit.summary: Most static shell, obfuscation, and path traversal findings are false positives caused by JavaScript template literals, ANSI escape sequences, imports, Markdown fences, and ellipsis text. Confirmed risks are a command skill that reads GUAIKEI_API_TOKEN, sends it to www.guaikei.com in URL query parameters, performs HTTPS requests, and writes local lock and result files. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious intent was found, but publication should warn users about third-party token transmission and local output files.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party API Token Passed in Query Parameters
medium_findings.description: The script reads GUAIKEI_API_TOKEN and the request builder places token in query parameters for both create and result requests to www.guaikei.com. This appears intended for API access, but query-string tokens can be captured in service logs, proxies, and error traces.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The data flow is direct: the environment token is read and then serialized into request query parameters. Confidence is below very high because the token is for the same third-party API the skill is designed to call.

medium_findings.title: User-Derived Local Files and Result Persistence
medium_findings.description: The command creates lock files from the sanitized keyword and writes last-search.json under the scripts directory. Sanitization removes path separators, so path traversal was not confirmed, but persisted search results may expose user research on shared machines.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are explicit and use user-derived keyword data. The impact is moderate because paths are constrained to the script directory and slash characters are removed during sanitization.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Default API Token
low_findings.description: When GUAIKEI_API_TOKEN is missing or invalid, the key module returns a fixed default token. The weak-cryptography static finding is a false positive, but a shared fallback token can create rate-limit, attribution, and reliability issues.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The fallback token behavior is visible in the code. It is not cryptographic use, so the security impact is lower than the static scanner suggested.

low_findings.title: Documentation Overstates Data Desensitization
low_findings.description: The documentation says all output data is desensitized and has no personal information, but the code can output author nicknames and profile URLs returned by the API. This is a marketplace content and privacy expectation issue rather than hidden exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The mismatch is clear from the documentation and output formatting code. The risk is limited because the skill appears to process public Xiaohongshu data returned by its API provider.

low_findings.title: Static Command and Obfuscation Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: No evidence of shell execution, eval, subprocess spawning, or prompt injection was found in reviewed files. The scanner matched JavaScript template literals, ANSI color escape sequences, relative imports, Markdown code fences, Unicode ranges, and ellipsis text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations contain formatting, imports, or documentation examples rather than executable shell commands or encoded payloads. No untrusted instruction text attempts to override the audit.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment Token Serialized into HTTPS Query String
dangerous_patterns.description: GUAIKEI_API_TOKEN is read from the environment and included in request query parameters. This should be documented and preferably moved to an authorization header by the maintainer.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The token handling is explicit in the script and request construction. It is risky primarily because query strings are commonly logged.

dangerous_patterns.title: Persistent Local Output File
dangerous_patterns.description: The command writes search results to scripts/last-search.json after printing output. Users should know that searches and returned public profile links may remain on disk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write operation and included result fields are visible in the command. The risk depends on whether the user runs the skill on a shared or sensitive system.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded Third-Party API Host
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill is tied to www.guaikei.com for task creation and result retrieval. This is expected behavior for the skill, but users must trust that third-party service with search terms and the API token.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The host and request paths are hardcoded and used for core functionality. No evidence suggests unrelated exfiltration, so this is a trust dependency rather than confirmed abuse.

content.value_statement: Xiaohongshu research can take hours when teams manually inspect notes, creators, and engagement signals. This skill searches public notes by keyword and returns structured results for faster content and market analysis.
actual_capabilities: Search Xiaohongshu public notes by keyword through the Guaikei API.
Filter results by content type: all, video, or image-text notes.
Sort results by comprehensive ranking, newest, likes, comments, or saves.
Limit returned results from 1 to 60 items.
Return results as JSON or a Markdown summary.
Save the latest successful result set to a local last-search.json file.
limitations: Requires Node.js 16 or later and network access to www.guaikei.com.
Requires GUAIKEI_API_TOKEN for reliable use; the fallback token may be limited.
Only returns fields provided by the third-party API for public Xiaohongshu content.
Does not verify engagement authenticity, creator identity, or platform compliance by itself.

use_cases.title: Find Content Ideas
use_cases.description: Search a topic and review high-engagement notes before planning new Xiaohongshu posts.

use_cases.title: Monitor Competitor Topics
use_cases.description: Track branded or category keywords to see recent public notes and engagement signals.

use_cases.title: Build Research Snapshots
use_cases.description: Collect structured keyword results for quick trend checks and campaign planning.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Keyword Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Xiaohongshu for "summer skincare" and return 10 results in JSON format.

prompt_templates.title: Video Trend Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Xiaohongshu videos for "home workout" and sort by newest results.

prompt_templates.title: High Engagement Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Search "camping gear" on Xiaohongshu, return 20 image-text notes, and sort by likes.

prompt_templates.title: Competitor Monitoring Snapshot
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Xiaohongshu for our competitor brand keyword, return 30 results in Markdown, and summarize titles, links, creators, and engagement patterns.

output_examples.input: Search for "AI tutorial" with 10 comprehensive results.
output_examples.output: A ranked result list with note titles, publish times, note links, creator names, and engagement counts.

output_examples.input: Search for "spring outfits" as image-text notes sorted by likes.
output_examples.output: A Markdown summary showing the highest-liked public notes and their visible engagement metrics.

output_examples.input: Search for a brand keyword and save the latest results.
output_examples.output: Console output plus a local last-search.json file containing the most recent structured result set.

best_practices: Use a private GUAIKEI_API_TOKEN and rotate it if logs may expose query strings.
Keep keywords specific and between 2 and 50 characters for cleaner results.
Delete last-search.json when search results should not remain on disk.
anti_patterns: Do not search private, sensitive, or personally identifying terms without a valid business reason.
Do not treat returned engagement counts as verified fraud-free analytics.
Do not publish results without checking Xiaohongshu platform terms and data-use rules.

faq.question: Does this skill scrape Xiaohongshu directly?
faq.answer: No. The code sends keyword search requests to the Guaikei API and formats the returned results.

faq.question: What credentials does it need?
faq.answer: It expects GUAIKEI_API_TOKEN in the environment. A built-in fallback token exists but may be limited.

faq.question: Where does the token go?
faq.answer: The token is sent to www.guaikei.com in HTTPS query parameters for search task creation and retrieval.

faq.question: Does it store results locally?
faq.answer: Yes. After a successful search, it writes the latest result set to scripts/last-search.json.

faq.question: Can it analyze KOL quality automatically?
faq.answer: It can return creator and engagement fields from search results, but it does not verify audience quality or authenticity.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code when the runtime requirements are available.
